page: Amazon Web Services c8g.24xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor built on the arm64 architecture. The instance features 96 dedicated physical cores and 96 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz, with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. Cache specifications include 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. The instance runs on the Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps. No GPU accelerators are present.

In benchmark evaluations, the c8g.24xlarge demonstrates strong multi-core performance, placing in the top tier for stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench multi-core, and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression. Memory bandwidth is highly competitive, scoring in the top tier for both small-block cached and large-block uncached read and write operations. However, single-core performance represents a relative tradeoff, with stress-ng single-core and gzip single-threaded compression performing in the average tier. For specialized workloads, the instance achieves top-tier results in Passmark database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput, alongside top-tier text generation speeds for medium and large language models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the arm64-based Graviton architecture delivers high multi-core compute density and memory bandwidth without the premium associated with traditional x86 architectures. The absence of local storage and GPU hardware makes this instance highly specialized for non-graphical, memory-bandwidth-sensitive, and parallelized CPU tasks. The c8g.24xlarge is suited for multi-threaded workloads such as software compilation, high-throughput database operations, static web serving, Redis caching layers, and CPU-based LLM text generation.
description: Amazon Web Services c8g.24xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance powered by a 2.8 GHz AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. It delivers 96 dedicated physical cores with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio, paired with 192.0 GB of memory and a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmarks reveal top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and high-throughput database and Redis operations, though single-core execution speeds remain in the average tier. This resource profile offers high compute density and qualitative cost efficiency for parallelized workloads. It is suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, static web serving, database operations, and CPU-based language model text generation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8g.24xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance featuring 96 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 192 GB RAM, and 40 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core performance.

tagline: An arm64 compute-optimized platform featuring ninety-six dedicated cores and high memory bandwidth for parallelized, high-throughput workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 96 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 arm64 cores at 2.8 GHz.
Features 192.0 GB of system memory and 40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance.
Lacks local storage and GPU hardware, optimizing cost for pure compute workloads.
Best suited for software compilation, high-throughput databases, and web serving.
